rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JASON LHOTKA BY ELENA LHOTKA, et al VS. GEORAPHIC EXPEDITONS, INC., ALSO DOING BUSINESS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Geographic Expeditions, Inc, Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 20, 2011, line 2, Defendant Geographic Expeditions, Inc. Motion For Summary Judgment. NOTE: The Court is considering the Motion as a Motion for Summary Adjudication to the Third Amended Complaint. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication to Plaintiff¿s 6th cause of action (declaratory Relief) is granted. Because this Court had previously denied Defendant¿s Motion to Compel Arbitration and Defendant has exhausted its appeals, the cause of action for declaratory relief no longer presents an actual controversy. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to whether primary assumption of risk applies is denied. A triable issue of material fact exists about whether dying of HACE and/or HAPE is an inherent risk associated with climbing Mt. Kilimanjaro. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the release of liability is denied. This Court has already found that both substantive and procedural unconscionability permeates the agreement and this decision was upheld on appeal. In light of this ruling, and in analyzing the contract as a whole, the Court cannot enforce the contract. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to its argument that it is not liable for the acts of the guides because they were employees of African Outdoor Expeditions is denied. A triable issue of material fact exists as to whether defendants caused Plaintiffs to believe that the guides were employee or agent of Defendant and whether Plaintiffs reasonably relied on these representations. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the 1st and 2nd causes of action (misrepresentation) are denied. There are tirable issues of material fact as to whether Defendant knew its representations were false, as to whether Defendant intended to defraud plaintiff and reliance on the representation by Plaintiff. Defendant¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication as to the 3rd cause of action (IIED) is denied. Plaintiff has presented evidence t

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM A. WINEBERG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration/ For Relief From Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 20, 2011, line 6, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM WINEBERG'S Motion For Reconsideration/ For Relief From Order. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO AMEND ITS ORIGINAL ORDER, SPECIFICALLY THE PORTION WHERE THE COURT STRIKING THE PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION. HOWEVER, THE COURT DID CONSIDER PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IN REACHING ITS ORIGINAL DECISION AND, THEREFORE, FINDS NO BASIS TO RECONSIDER ITS ORIGINAL DECISION OR CHANGE IT OTHER THAN INDICATED ABOVE. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: MK ENTERPRISES INC. VS. JAMES EASON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Vacating Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 20, 2011, line 17, PETITIONER MK ENTERPRISES INC.'S Verified Petition For Order Vacating Arbitration Award. PETITIONER'S MOTION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT ACT IN EXCESS OF HER POWERS. MOREOVER, THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT IMPROPERLY EXCLUDE EVIDENCE. RESPONDENT'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE REQUEST WAS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER. RESPONDENT MUST FILE A SEPARATE MOTION FOR FEES AND COSTS AFTER THE COURT HEARS AND GRANTS ITS PETITION TO CONFIRM THE ARBITRATION AWARD. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE M. ZOLOT VS. NORTHWESTERN MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 20, 2011, line 19, DEFENDANT NORTHWESTERN MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLEAD HIS CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND DEFENDANT'S REMAINING GROUNDS ON DEMURRER ARE MORE APPROPRIATELY DETERMINED ON A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. BASED ON THE ABOVE THE DEMURRER ON THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IS OVERRULED AS WELL. =(302/LMG)
